sanskrit:
यान्ति देवव्रता देवान् पितॄन् यान्ति पितृव्रता: |
भूतानि यान्ति भूतेज्या यान्ति मद्याजिनोऽपि माम् ||
transliteration:
yānti deva-vratā devān pitṝn yānti pitṛ-vratāḥ |
bhūtāni yānti bhūtejyā yānti mad-yājino 'pi mām ||
literal_meaning: Worshipers of the gods go to the gods; ancestor-worshipers go to the ancestors; ghost-worshipers go to the ghosts; those who worship Me come to Me.
contextual_meaning: Your attention is a seed; whatever you repeatedly invest it in becomes the garden you eventually inhabit.  Krishna maps four common life-investments (celestial success, ancestral belonging, occult power, or divine love) to their corresponding inner destinations, showing that identity is less about belief than about the object of sustained emotional energy.

shadow: Terror of being 'stuck' with wrong object

psychological_state: Cognitive re-appraisal of life goals; shift from extrinsic to intrinsic valuation; post-conventional identity formation.
trigger_scenario: A college sophomore scrolls LinkedIn, feels FOMO seeing peers chasing prestige, startups, or spiritual retreats.  The verse reframes the anxiety: 'Where is my daily attention actually deposited?  That, not the headline, writes my future self.'
anchor_text: You become what you steady your heart upon; choose your love and it will choose your future.
